results.title: PM Modi launches ₹1 Lakh Crore R&D Fund at ESTIC 2025
results.content: Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. Generation Lab just raised $11M in seed funding, led by Accel, marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by Alina Rui Su, Dr. Irina Conboy, and Michael Suswal, Generation Lab isn't just talking about #aging; they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, Harvard Medical School PhD, University of California, Berkeley bioengineering alum, Forbes 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped [...] professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped #agingbiology on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of Standard AI and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your #biologicalage, it measures it. With over 460 #epigenetic markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your #brain, #heart, #liver, or #immunesystem really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year #brainage reversal using MUSE #Stemcell treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, [...] featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, Giannis Antetokounmpo's BYL Ventures, and Simu Liu's Markham Valley Ventures, pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on #longevity as the next frontier. When George Church, Aubrey de Grey, and Dr. Adeel Khan, MD are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline. In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade #diagnostics built to turn #healthspan into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't. The play here isn't

# PM Modi launches ₹1 Lakh Crore R&D Fund at ESTIC 2025

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[Sai Kumar Reddy Midde](https://in.linkedin.com/in/sai-kumar-reddy-midde?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

* [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsai-kumar-reddy-midde_estic2025-innovation-youthinscience-activity-7390738433721368576-BPuq&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

A dream moment for every young innovator in India! ✨ PM Modi is launching a ₹1 Lakh Crore Research and Development Fund at ESTIC 2025 to power new ideas, deep tech research, and global scale innovation. This visionary initiative will fuel breakthroughs across Artificial Intelligence, Quantum Technology, Space, Clean Energy, and Health Science, turning today’s experiments into tomorrow’s revolutions. ESTIC 2025 (Emerging Science, Technology and Innovation Conclave) will be held from 3rd to 5th November 2025 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. The conclave will feature plenary talks, 11 thematic sessions, 35 deep tech startup showcases, and 100 poster presentations, uniting Nobel Laureates, young scientists, entrepreneurs, and policymakers to shape the future of Indian innovation. So proud to see India investing in the minds that will shape our future. 🚀 [#ESTIC2025](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Festic2025&trk=public_post-text) [#Innovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finnovation&trk=public_post-text) [#YouthInScience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fyouthinscience&trk=public_post-text) [#R](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fr&trk=public_post-text)&D [#ViksitBharat](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fviksitbharat&trk=public_post-text) [#AatmanirbharBharat](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faatmanirbharbharat&trk=public_post-text) [#ScienceAndTechnology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fscienceandtechnology&trk=public_post-text) [#IndiaRising](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Findiarising&trk=public_post-text)

  A Reality Check from ESTIC 2025 – Time to Rethink What Truly Matters! At ESTIC 2025, organized by DST, Government of India, I kept hearing powerful, purposeful words: Innovation, Translational Research, Start-up, Fundamental Research, Biomanufacturing, Idea, Prototype, Policy, Patent, Social Contribution, National Priority, Viksit Bharat, Research, Development & Innovation (RDI), BioE3, Industry Collaboration, Regulatory Affairs, Private Sector-driven, Inclusivity....... and so on. But you know what I didn’t hear? ❌ h-index. ❌ Top 2% scientist (by citation) list. ❌ Impact factor. Neither I could trace the selction process of Young Science & Technology Leaders' and 'Senior Science & Technology Leaders' was driven by the above metrices. And that silence spoke louder than any keynote. While we’re still busy glorifying citation counts and journal metrics, the world — and India — has clearly moved on. The focus is shifting from publishing papers to building purpose, from counting citations to creating change. If we, as scientists and educators, keep chasing vanity metrics instead of national missions, we risk becoming irrelevant to the very society we’re supposed to serve. It’s time we ask ourselves hard questions: 👉 Are we solving India’s problems or just publishing about them/ hot-topics? 👉 Are we mentoring innovators or just measuring impact factors? 👉 Are we contributing to national growth — or to citation databases? Let’s stop endorsing what doesn’t align with India’s priorities. Let’s build what matters — solutions, not statistics. Note: The voice is personal, neither institutional nor with an expectation to be endorsed by all. The post is not intended to disrespect any scientists/ scientific community having extraordinary research output, its just about the focus/ priority. Would love to hear from fellow researchers, innovators, and policy thinkers — Is it time to redefine what “research excellence” means for India?   Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) just raised $11M in seed funding, led by [Accel](https://www.linkedin.com/company/accel-vc?trk=public_post-text), marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text), Dr. Irina Conboy, and [Michael Suswal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-suswal-51b45421?trk=public_post-text), Generation Lab isn't just talking about [#aging](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faging&trk=public_post-text); they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) PhD, [University of California, Berkeley](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uc-berkeley/?trk=public_post-text) bioengineering alum, [Forbes](https://www.linkedin.com/company/forbes-magazine?trk=public_post-text) 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose [#research](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fresearch&trk=public_post-text) on plasma dilution flipped [#agingbiology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fagingbiology&trk=public_post-text) on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of [Standard AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/standard-ai?trk=public_post-text) and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your [#biologicalage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiologicalage&trk=public_post-text), it measures it. With over 460 [#epigenetic](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fepigenetic&trk=public_post-text) markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your [#brain](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrain&trk=public_post-text), [#heart](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fheart&trk=public_post-text), [#liver](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fliver&trk=public_post-text), or [#immunesystem](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimmunesystem&trk=public_post-text) really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year [#brainage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrainage&trk=public_post-text) reversal using MUSE [#Stemcell](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstemcell&trk=public_post-text) treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring [Samsung Next](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samsung-next?trk=public_post-text), [Zone2](https://www.linkedin.com/company/z2-zone2?trk=public_post-text), Steve Aoki's [Aoki Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/aokilabs?trk=public_post-text), Giannis Antetokounmpo's [BYL Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bylventures?trk=public_post-text), and [Simu Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simu-liu-47946a2b1?trk=public_post-text)'s [Markham Valley Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/markhamvalley?trk=public_post-text), pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on [#longevity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flongevity&trk=public_post-text) as the next frontier. When George Church, [Aubrey de Grey](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aubrey-de-grey-24260b?trk=public_post-text), and [Dr. Adeel Khan, MD](https://ae.linkedin.com/in/dradeelkhan-md?trk=public_post-text) are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline. In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade [#diagnostics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdiagnostics&trk=public_post-text) built to turn [#healthspan](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhealthspan&trk=public_post-text) into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't. The play here isn't immortality, it's accountability. Knowing how your organs are aging before symptoms show up changes the entire conversation around healthcare. Generation Lab isn't selling forever; they're selling control. That's the kind of disruption Accel just paid attention to.   The Research and Development Innovation Fund sets a new precedent in the Government of India's policy framework. Not only does it provide funds for translational research and deeptech products, but is a market shaping instrument crowding in industry RnD investments and LP money for deeptech startups. The government lays the framework and financing but let experts and market make the allocation decisions. This is a masterstroke. [Ashish Dhawan](https://in.linkedin.com/in/ashish-dhawan-241112?trk=public_post-text) and I write about it in today's Financial Express. It is now for the ecosystem to respond and make use of this enabling policy, towards making India a top 3 science and technology nation.   I read the 103-page Deeptech Report 2025 by [Kae Capital](https://in.linkedin.com/company/kaecapital?trk=public_post-text) x [TDK Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tdk-ventures?trk=public_post-text), so you don’t have to. ➡️ India is at a Deeptech inflection point. ➡️ Breakthroughs everywhere from Semiconductors, Space, Energy, and Quantum. ➡️ $1.3B raised in 2024 with 78% YoY growth. Deeptech-focused and sector-agnostic funds are rising fast. 300+ high-research labs across the nation. Returnee talent from NASA, SpaceX, and Intel. India is building from Earth to the Edge. ➡️ On the Ground, which transforms Core Sectors 1. Next-Gen Energy Tech $225B by 2033. Innovation across Energy generation, distribution, storage, and consumption. Major Players: Ather ($630M+), Exponent ($25M), Ultraviolette ($150M+), SolarSquare ($61M). 2. Robotics & Automation +59% YoY growth. (i) Upstream – Sensors, IoT. (ii) Midstream – Robot arms, actuators. (iii) Downstream – E-commerce, retail, supply chain. Major Players: Addverb ($67M), GreyOrange ($180M+), Svaya Robotics ($15M). 3. B2C Deeptech Smart rings, wearables, and home robots. Major Players: Ultrahuman ($55M), Miko ($93M), Atomberg ($127M). From Make in India to global products. 4. Cybersecurity Market projected at $13B by 2030. India is the 5th most breached nation. Use cases: Hardware, software, enterprise security. Major Player: Druva ($475M). 5. Agritech 1,000+ startups using AI, IoT, and drones. Use cases: soil health, precision farming, seed analytics. Major Players: DeHaat ($270M), Ninjacart ($350M), Fasal ($50M), Plantix. ➡️ Critical Foundations: Building the Base 1. Advanced Materials $5B in 2021, expected to double by 2028. Graphene, composites, nanomaterials. Major Players: Log9 ($50M), Atomgrid, Scimplify Tech. 2. Semiconductors ₹76,000 Cr Semicon India push. Major Players: Signalchip ($60M), Q-NU, Kaynes Technology. ➡️ Beyond Boundaries: Pushing Frontiers in Space 1. SpaceTech Launch vehicles, ground stations, debris management. Major Players: Pixxel ($71M), Skyroot ($100M+), Agnikul ($40M). IN-SPACe has lowered entry barriers with a ₹500 Cr fund. 2. Quantum Still early, but real. Major Players: Quafluence, QπAi. Use cases: hardware, encryption, software optimisation. ➡️ Deeptech 2025: The Overview 1,600+ startups. 2.3× growth since 2019. $1.3B funding in 2024. 25+ Deeptech-focused VCs. Top hubs: Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, Chennai. Median Series A timeline is about 5.2 years. Access to capital is improving; regulations are easing. Product adoption maturing; exits are real, ex-Ather IPO, Ola IPO, multiple M&A cases. Despite challenges across funding, talent, and infrastructure, founders remain optimistic about India’s Deeptech future. What do you think are the main constraints in building Deeptech startups in India: capital, talent, or market openness ?? # SCET Alum and Berkeley Professor Launch Generation Lab for Personalized Anti-Aging

Berkeley Bioengineering Prof. Irina Conboy, Michael Suswal and SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su [...] Berkeley Bioengineering Prof. Irina Conboy, Michael Suswal and SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su

SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su, and University of California, Berkeley Bioengineering Professor Irina Conboy are co-founding Generation Lab with the Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today

Generation Lab will offer the first test based on biomedical evidence that measures an individual’s biological age progression, officially launched today out of stealth. The company is announcing its pre-seed funding and the opening of its test kit waitlist. SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su, and University of California, Berkeley Bioengineering Professor Irina Conboy are co-founding Generation Lab with the Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today

# **SCET Alum and Berkeley Professor Launch Generation Lab for Personalized Anti-Aging**

[![Berkeley Bioengineering Prof. Irina Conboy, Michael Suswal and SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su](https://scet.berkeley.edu/wp-content/uploads/DSC02801-300x200.jpg)](https://scet.berkeley.edu/scet-alum-and-berkeley-professor-launch-generation-lab-for-personalized-anti-aging/ "SCET Alum and Berkeley Professor Launch Generation Lab for Personalized Anti-Aging")

**SCET Alum and [former Collider Cup](https://scet.berkeley.edu/collider-cup-profile-alina-su-ceo-founder-of-novaxs-biotech-corp-winner-of-pad-13-pitch-competition-member-of-berkeley-skydeck-program-and-1st-place-of-berkeley-fall-2021-collider-cup/) winner, Alina Su, and University of California, Berkeley Bioengineering Professor Irina Conboy are co-founding Generation Lab with the** **Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today**

[Generation Lab](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=TeZUXWpUv-2B6TCY38pVLo9nqCzlsziu-2F4wh5D2NlmVo9uL4RE6RyHrpPEhkFUxj8rHV6T_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioB280ksQbNBb8yCQth9i-2BjwifrcC0BGg1m5MgDd79GDbPPPLvnZ0Si1pKTGAmeTR4YNnjb7pC2XPr9vrCpxx74g41iDhTDaASDIJ58tCxW-2B8PR-2F0s1UKP7vYzbLO46-2FWZDR1hup1PFZ-2BrxMhh8XLxI-3D) will offer the first test based on biomedical evidence that measures an individual’s biological age progression, officially launched today out of stealth. The company is announcing its pre-seed funding and the opening of its [test kit waitlist](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=CVi7es-2FCNK-2FU4f58lHsDTlDj1TlDMlIK48xsd3z0C-2BA-3DZoqj_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joiuwcyerl-2FsPn4Y6mPgzeyYHHmKvmQ7w0dDw-2B3zOdFAPJHnr9b7kUXavh7Sp2xwWAmEd0CR9OZgw0fX7HOd7TcYf0-2BlAM9tMwGgVJQ94iTVwjJTFV-2B1hFCrJ0eHwKfZMK7MasE-2F9pLVZplCiGQhkNuy4-3D).

Co-founded by [Irina Conboy](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUU2LHKSbPg854wTzRTSv0e9PJ582xD6DNSESk2gn6QZaexS60B0sE2Zi9kyeK2jSBQ-3D-3DBHg5_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioJD040nHobNbpWz3V5eSPxVO0LZnsm0kHydeleYuGZWPAqLGD-2FWNnuMi78Y3hx-2FJJs7syIuUdGHZgTzLq0IeYHUkTq3kHAojLc41S7LnQF6xFlpjM1CvwfSNSR66Uv5gq7prLU1SDZd7kqOHJAnDKs-3D), a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time.

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

[George Church, Ph. D](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUdXTV3iQG28JGooOhy7cWzK92SocBokvhLFK6sB33ZQgpoi7p8Qlun30qw4VYNguOZoVNWv6gPcBqcnbfXxbMRQ-3DYiY0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joig4aeR72jOsxLtXKgAH76cI7dD1E-2FZo0r1b6sRHAf2grux18hcZ2XVWT9FthMeqfDyOGrDFUD7fMo6e2TnIKHNV45JCbtvf99DMKv9tdHE2W-2BAX5jlWzc6OiyDQjW-2FqpygTXEBo0Zo4y1TxIb-2BuBq4A-3D)., the Robert Winthrop Professor of Genetics at Harvard Medical School, is a renowned geneticist who is serving as an advisor to Generation Lab. “Irina and her team are taking cutting edge research straight out of the lab and applying it to a pressing issue: identifying disease much earlier than ever before possible using genetic markers,” said Dr. Church. “Seeing this unfold, and having a front-row seat to its impact, is going to be a pivotal moment in epigenetics – it’s incredibly exciting.”

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

Generation Lab’s other two co-founders are [Alina Rui Su](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUegha2L32XA5BGAQD-2FrrWT8ik1GwoEXnvGpGYc2nrM18TU4N_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoisM8XpuULqO1EOlO7whnQKgDfuutkYV8iNDIuuXaaNxIeNiGySaHts-2BOY8mibNTnXhhYJCWb7WQnV3eicUCcIvQwY3p7ss-2FZQ-2Ba24xxu0LyMjFwIUhcJgZbik2E27w-2BRVSK-2B1hm-2FXk2f5LggQRB-2Fmco-3D) and [Michael Suswal](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUegha2L32XA5BGAQD-2FrrWT-2FuMsN-2FvBofucFSDTFfKWPfrn49-2FbNt8DeQtyIvnpeQDQ-3D-3DOLIN_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoiiOiLgj4laIyYQqUlrv6DFacKhLUkeM5u-2F-2BpJ1fm5-2FlekiR26YXUQZrwohjCU31Bd0GNhCRJ1X4Pd7PXaPLnjpaPStkqp-2Bpu0PstT0ZkUWQtWMGyl3OH-2BHbDGn0iF4UWBKpAogFdrSujYkO3lSc-2FmRQ-3D). Su, who studied longevity at Harvard Medical School and Berkeley, was previously founder and CEO of NovaXS. She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

[George Church, Ph. D](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUdXTV3iQG28JGooOhy7cWzK92SocBokvhLFK6sB33ZQgpoi7p8Qlun30qw4VYNguOZoVNWv6gPcBqcnbfXxbMRQ-3DYiY0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joig4aeR72jOsxLtXKgAH76cI7dD1E-2FZo0r1b6sRHAf2grux18hcZ2XVWT9FthMeqfDyOGrDFUD7fMo6e2TnIKHNV45JCbtvf99DMKv9tdHE2W-2BAX5jlWzc6OiyDQjW-2FqpygTXEBo0Zo4y1TxIb-2BuBq4A-3D)., the Robert Winthrop Professor of Genetics at Harvard Medical School, is a renowned geneticist who is serving as an advisor to Generation Lab. “Irina and her team are taking cutting edge research straight out of the lab and applying it to a pressing issue: identifying disease much earlier than ever before possible using genetic markers,” said Dr. Church. “Seeing this unfold, and having a front-row seat to its impact, is going to be a pivotal moment in epigenetics – it’s incredibly exciting.”

Su, who studied longevity at Harvard Medical School and Berkeley, was previously founder and CEO of NovaXS. She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).

**About Generation Lab**

[Generation Lab](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUZrSG-2B8zH2SR-2BHQTMN36DZ3f4kRqkqiRUJIcd34cBlV7Yvw4uIvDqFlNuwg6MsVRSlIUcCd3f5lIMHknULj9Y3bn0z2QECM81KHEMp642mo95jlI_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoiiejjKfhfOTaNi7sgN2k-2BkoS6k5LKzPaOUn5WKqUmS4yE3o6dldQYIsOBtTHzxdJBY74B4ZyaJy7upOKimoSRKOpGoqWofKbj80Xx8qJonQURleD5frLyFojWytbzqgEsiT9-2FSEl9MGRnXAt-2BeqX2cg-3D) aims to extend the human healthspan with the first test based on clinically relevant biological evidence that measures individuals’ biological age progression and risk of disease so everyone can live longer, healthier lives. Co-founded by a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time. Join the waitlist at[Link to SendGrid](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUZrSG-2B8zH2SR-2BHQTMN36DZ08cdXEblIBO2XwadJsBV8-2BuEu-2FudScAqGA2q1b-2FGp7XfFpOzpZ-2F39TIR1MEuuUrLA-3D-nAc_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoiskaBlGBf0sUPhiIMEy7S3axvXSYJN6LUABQY0vqzlQY-2F-2FgZN-2Fp-2B8OSfRf-2BAMxKk8Fb2K2cmgcZ3hrGreBw-2B4As4ZfnXrwav913XGqYxzi1McLw1AaB6KXeuVjuLk3A4E9pu5WcJpRO95t8pmrAEgkU-3D)[generationlab.co](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUZrSG-2B8zH2SR-2BHQTMN36DZ08cdXEblIBO2XwadJsBV8-2BuEu-2FudScAqGA2q1b-2FGp7XfFpOzpZ-2F39TIR1MEuuUrLA-3Dnugo_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoikoPz4g4aI9aq9Ez8zqsfmO20xsdPTDyLIZsF2MQWSiNwDDl1qFcvTTZGHvagyla-2F1BuHSQdOYrAGf-2F94-2FYTk7TU2oDBSslu7WBJpmBPQcgxCF2ba0zBBTg45VemZ39JnLjFQRtj8j72cFa-2BXyd3PZA-3D).

![Berkeley Bioengineering Prof. Irina Conboy, Michael Suswal and SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su](https://scet.berkeley.edu/wp-content/uploads/DSC02801-1024x683.jpg)

**SCET Alum and [former Collider Cup](https://scet.berkeley.edu/collider-cup-profile-alina-su-ceo-founder-of-novaxs-biotech-corp-winner-of-pad-13-pitch-competition-member-of-berkeley-skydeck-program-and-1st-place-of-berkeley-fall-2021-collider-cup/) winner, Alina Su, and University of California, Berkeley Bioengineering Professor Irina Conboy are co-founding Generation Lab with the** **Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today**

[Generation Lab](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=TeZUXWpUv-2B6TCY38pVLo9nqCzlsziu-2F4wh5D2NlmVo9uL4RE6RyHrpPEhkFUxj8rHV6T_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioB280ksQbNBb8yCQth9i-2BjwifrcC0BGg1m5MgDd79GDbPPPLvnZ0Si1pKTGAmeTR4YNnjb7pC2XPr9vrCpxx74g41iDhTDaASDIJ58tCxW-2B8PR-2F0s1UKP7vYzbLO46-2FWZDR1hup1PFZ-2BrxMhh8XLxI-3D) will offer the first test based on biomedical evidence that measures an individual’s biological age progression, officially launched today out of stealth. The company is announcing its pre-seed funding and the opening of its [test kit waitlist](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=CVi7es-2FCNK-2FU4f58lHsDTlDj1TlDMlIK48xsd3z0C-2BA-3DZoqj_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joiuwcyerl-2FsPn4Y6mPgzeyYHHmKvmQ7w0dDw-2B3zOdFAPJHnr9b7kUXavh7Sp2xwWAmEd0CR9OZgw0fX7HOd7TcYf0-2BlAM9tMwGgVJQ94iTVwjJTFV-2B1hFCrJ0eHwKfZMK7MasE-2F9pLVZplCiGQhkNuy4-3D).

Co-founded by [Irina Conboy](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUU2LHKSbPg854wTzRTSv0e9PJ582xD6DNSESk2gn6QZaexS60B0sE2Zi9kyeK2jSBQ-3D-3DBHg5_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioJD040nHobNbpWz3V5eSPxVO0LZnsm0kHydeleYuGZWPAqLGD-2FWNnuMi78Y3hx-2FJJs7syIuUdGHZgTzLq0IeYHUkTq3kHAojLc41S7LnQF6xFlpjM1CvwfSNSR66Uv5gq7prLU1SDZd7kqOHJAnDKs-3D), a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time.

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

[George Church, Ph. D](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUdXTV3iQG28JGooOhy7cWzK92SocBokvhLFK6sB33ZQgpoi7p8Qlun30qw4VYNguOZoVNWv6gPcBqcnbfXxbMRQ-3DYiY0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joig4aeR72jOsxLtXKgAH76cI7dD1E-2FZo0r1b6sRHAf2grux18hcZ2XVWT9FthMeqfDyOGrDFUD7fMo6e2TnIKHNV45JCbtvf99DMKv9tdHE2W-2BAX5jlWzc6OiyDQjW-2FqpygTXEBo0Zo4y1TxIb-2BuBq4A-3D)., the Robert Winthrop Professor of Genetics at Harvard Medical School, is a renowned geneticist who is serving as an advisor to Generation Lab. “Irina and her team are taking cutting edge research straight out of the lab and applying it to a pressing issue: identifying disease much earlier than ever before possible using genetic markers,” said Dr. Church. “Seeing this unfold, and having a front-row seat to its impact, is going to be a pivotal moment in epigenetics – it’s incredibly exciting.”

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

Generation Lab’s other two co-founders are [Alina Rui Su](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUegha2L32XA5BGAQD-2FrrWT8ik1GwoEXnvGpGYc2nrM18TU4N_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoisM8XpuULqO1EOlO7whnQKgDfuutkYV8iNDIuuXaaNxIeNiGySaHts-2BOY8mibNTnXhhYJCWb7WQnV3eicUCcIvQwY3p7ss-2FZQ-2Ba24xxu0LyMjFwIUhcJgZbik2E27w-2BRVSK-2B1hm-2FXk2f5LggQRB-2Fmco-3D) and [Michael Suswal](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUegha2L32XA5BGAQD-2FrrWT-2FuMsN-2FvBofucFSDTFfKWPfrn49-2FbNt8DeQtyIvnpeQDQ-3D-3DOLIN_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoiiOiLgj4laIyYQqUlrv6DFacKhLUkeM5u-2F-2BpJ1fm5-2FlekiR26YXUQZrwohjCU31Bd0GNhCRJ1X4Pd7PXaPLnjpaPStkqp-2Bpu0PstT0ZkUWQtWMGyl3OH-2BHbDGn0iF4UWBKpAogFdrSujYkO3lSc-2FmRQ-3D). Su, who studied longevity at Harvard Medical School and Berkeley, was previously founder and CEO of NovaXS. She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).

Co-founded by a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time. ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6643a08d305ab77f8c7566b6/670819c9dc53f9c02fe60190_logo-accel.png)

Portfolio News

Published

Oct 22, 2025

# Creating the Standard for Longevity: Our Seed Investment in Generation Lab

written by

Kerry Wang

![Creating the Standard for Longevity: Our Seed Investment in Generation Lab](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6661f5618fe795fa8894d04f/68f93bc5edba3321dc1e204f_Generation%20Lab%20Blog.avif)

For decades, aging has been treated as an inevitability. We’ve had plenty of wellness trends –  from cold plunges to juice cleanses – but little in the way of precise, science-backed tools to slow the biological clock, until now. The longevity market is growing fast, fueled by post-COVID health empowerment, high-profile biohackers like [Bryan Johnson](https://www.accel.com/relationships/braintree) (also an [Accel](https://www.accel.com/) founder), and the arrival of therapies that shift aging from “unavoidable decline” to “treatable condition.”

But to treat a problem, you need to measure it. Enter [Generation Lab](https://www.generationlab.com/), a company co-founded by renowned longevity researcher Dr. Irina Conboy, that is defining how we measure and eventually treat human aging.

Generation Lab’s SystemAge test uses a patented assay to analyze DNA methylation noise from a small at-home blood sample, producing an organ-by-organ biological age report. In addition, every individual test comes with personalized, science-backed recommendations including everything from therapeutic plasma exchange to supplements and workouts. Today, it’s the most accurate test on the market with 99.9% diagnostic accuracy, and trusted enough that over 275 clinics are already using it to recommend therapies and prove efficacy to patients. For me, the test surfaced a pre-existing condition that was invisible to a standard blood panel. I’m now proactively taking treatments, as well as giving the test to my entire family.

This is not another wellness app or one-size-fits-all health report. It’s a pick-and-shovel tool for the entire longevity ecosystem: clinicians, therapy providers, pharma R&D teams, and consumers at home. And it’s just the beginning. The team’s ambition is to make biological age testing as routine as a cholesterol panel and to leverage their science to develop anti-aging therapies themselves.

The founding trio behind Generation Lab is uniquely qualified to make this happen. CEO Alina Su is a Harvard PhD dropout and longevity lab researcher who combines scientific depth with relentless drive. CSO Irina Conboy is a tenured Berkeley professor and pioneer of the rejuvenating effects of parabiosis, bringing 20 years of groundbreaking aging research. COO Michael Suswal is a repeat unicorn hard-tech founder with a personal mission to extend healthy human lifespan. Their combination of cutting-edge science, operational excellence, and founder grit is rare.

Today, I’m thrilled to announce that [Accel](https://www.accel.com/) is leading Generation Lab’s seed financing. We couldn’t be more excited to partner with Alina, Michael, Irina, and the team on their charge to become the standard for longevity. If you want to extend your own healthspan, [use their test](https://www.generationlab.com/the-systemage-test?via=debda1) and [check out their open roles](https://www.generationlab.com/careers).

![Creating the Standard for Longevity: Our Seed Investment in Generation Lab](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6661f5618fe795fa8894d04f/68f93bc5edba3321dc1e204f_Generation%20Lab%20Blog.avif)

## Great companies aren't built alone.

Subscribe for tools, learnings, and updates from the Accel community.

results.content: Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. Generation Lab just raised $11M in seed funding, led by Accel, marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by Alina Rui Su, Dr. Irina Conboy, and Michael Suswal, Generation Lab isn't just talking about #aging; they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, Harvard Medical School PhD, University of California, Berkeley bioengineering alum, Forbes 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped [...] professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped #agingbiology on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of Standard AI and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your #biologicalage, it measures it. With over 460 #epigenetic markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your #brain, #heart, #liver, or #immunesystem really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year #brainage reversal using MUSE #Stemcell treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, [...] featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, Giannis Antetokounmpo's BYL Ventures, and Simu Liu's Markham Valley Ventures, pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on #longevity as the next frontier. When George Church, Aubrey de Grey, and Dr. Adeel Khan, MD are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline. In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade #diagnostics built to turn #healthspan into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't. The play here isn't

  🚀 Our latest ALL SBIR NSF I-Corps Cohort is Officially Underway! Last week, we launched our newest cohort of 17 deep tech startups across biotech, space, optics, materials, and AI. Over the next 7 weeks, these teams will complete 100+ customer discovery interviews to validate their go-to-market strategies and find true product-market fit. What makes this program transformative? We push teams beyond surface-level analysis to understand the actual jobs customers hire solutions to do. Our methodology helps founders see market validation signals clearly and teaches them to evaluate where to play, and what drives value. From therapeutic approaches to orbital cloud infrastructure for satellites —these innovators are solving real problems with deep science. The magic happens when brilliant technology meets rigorous customer discovery. That's where we come in. Excited to watch these teams transform their assumptions into validated business models.   + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fjesselandry23_aging-research-agingbiology-activity-7388644910641410048-5BzP&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

  Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) just raised $11M in seed funding, led by [Accel](https://www.linkedin.com/company/accel-vc?trk=public_post-text), marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text), Dr. Irina Conboy, and [Michael Suswal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-suswal-51b45421?trk=public_post-text), Generation Lab isn't just talking about [#aging](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faging&trk=public_post-text); they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) PhD, [University of California, Berkeley](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uc-berkeley/?trk=public_post-text) bioengineering alum, [Forbes](https://www.linkedin.com/company/forbes-magazine?trk=public_post-text) 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose [#research](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fresearch&trk=public_post-text) on plasma dilution flipped [#agingbiology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fagingbiology&trk=public_post-text) on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of [Standard AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/standard-ai?trk=public_post-text) and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your [#biologicalage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiologicalage&trk=public_post-text), it measures it. With over 460 [#epigenetic](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fepigenetic&trk=public_post-text) markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your [#brain](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrain&trk=public_post-text), [#heart](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fheart&trk=public_post-text), [#liver](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fliver&trk=public_post-text), or [#immunesystem](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimmunesystem&trk=public_post-text) really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year [#brainage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrainage&trk=public_post-text) reversal using MUSE [#Stemcell](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstemcell&trk=public_post-text) treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring [Samsung Next](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samsung-next?trk=public_post-text), [Zone2](https://www.linkedin.com/company/z2-zone2?trk=public_post-text), Steve Aoki's [Aoki Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/aokilabs?trk=public_post-text), Giannis Antetokounmpo's [BYL Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bylventures?trk=public_post-text), and [Simu Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simu-liu-47946a2b1?trk=public_post-text)'s [Markham Valley Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/markhamvalley?trk=public_post-text), pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on [#longevity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flongevity&trk=public_post-text) as the next frontier. When George Church, [Aubrey de Grey](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aubrey-de-grey-24260b?trk=public_post-text), and [Dr. Adeel Khan, MD](https://ae.linkedin.com/in/dradeelkhan-md?trk=public_post-text) are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline. In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade [#diagnostics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdiagnostics&trk=public_post-text) built to turn [#healthspan](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhealthspan&trk=public_post-text) into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't. The play here isn't immortality, it's accountability. Knowing how your organs are aging before symptoms show up changes the entire conversation around healthcare. Generation Lab isn't selling forever; they're selling control. That's the kind of disruption Accel just paid attention to.   Spend enough time around [#scientists](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fscientists&trk=public_post-text) and you see the paradox. They're mapping the brain like cartographers of consciousness, tearing into cancer cells with the precision of Formula 1 [#engineers](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fengineers&trk=public_post-text), yet half their workweek gets chewed up by messy data. Files scattered across labs, workflows duct-taped together, results that even the original team can't reproduce. That's not science, that's roulette. [DataJoint](https://www.linkedin.com/company/datajoint?trk=public_post-text) was built to kill that chaos. Not another "cloud tool," but a [#computationaldatabase](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcomputationaldatabase&trk=public_post-text) that turns life sciences data into something structured, automated, reproducible, and alive. Born in 2016 as Vathes LLC, rebranded in 2021, DataJoint was spun out of [Baylor College of Medicine](https://www.linkedin.com/school/baylor-college-of-medicine/?trk=public_post-text) by [Dimitri Yatsenko](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dimitriyatsenko?trk=public_post-text), PhD, and [Monty Kosma](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mkosma?trk=public_post-text). On 9/30/25, the Houston-based startup announced a $4.9M Seed round co-led by [Nina Capital](https://es.linkedin.com/company/ninacapital?trk=public_post-text) (Barcelona), [Inoca Capital Partners](https://www.linkedin.com/company/inoca-capital-partners?trk=public_post-text) (Salt Lake City), and [Capital Factory](https://www.linkedin.com/company/capital-factory?trk=public_post-text) (Austin), with follow-on support from existing investors. That's not just a check, it's validation from VCs who know [#healthcare](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhealthcare&trk=public_post-text), AI, and [#enterpriseSaaS](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fenterprisesaas&trk=public_post-text) cold. Leadership tells the story. [Jim Olson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jimolson?trk=public_post-text) stepped in as CEO in Jan '25, bringing the playbook that took Flywheel from startup to $100M+ in raised capital. Founder Dimitri Yatsenko, now Chief Science & Technology Officer, brings the deep [#neuroscience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fneuroscience&trk=public_post-text) and database chops that birthed the platform. Co-founder Monty Kosma, JD, bridges [#physics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fphysics&trk=public_post-text) and law into GTM strategy. Backing them is a tight crew of ~14 engineers, scientists, and operators grinding from Houston's Galleria to labs across the U.S. & Europe. The traction's already real. Within 6 months of launching publicly in early '24, DataJoint hit $500K ARR. Pipeline? $6M, with enterprise contracts averaging $500K+ ACV. Over 100 labs run their research on the platform, [Johns Hopkins Medicine](https://www.linkedin.com/company/johns-hopkins-medicine?trk=public_post-text), [Harvard University](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-university/?trk=public_post-text), [University of California, San Francisco](https://www.linkedin.com/school/ucsf/?trk=public_post-text), [UCL](https://uk.linkedin.com/school/university-college-london/?trk=public_post-text) to name a few. And now, with entry into the 9th [PharmStars](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pharmstars?trk=public_post-text) cohort, DataJoint is teeing up partnerships that plug directly into big [#pharma](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpharma&trk=public_post-text) and [#biotech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiotech&trk=public_post-text). The $4.9M raise fuels the next phase: scaling SaaS ops, locking down [#SOC2](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsoc2&trk=public_post-text)/ [#HIPAA](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhipaa&trk=public_post-text)/ [#ISO27001](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fiso27001&trk=public_post-text) compliance, expanding AI-driven workflows, and giving pharma-grade rigor to academic science. The core differentiator? Harmonizing [#multimodal](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmultimodal&trk=public_post-text) data, [#imaging](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimaging&trk=public_post-text), [#genomics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fgenomics&trk=public_post-text), [#electrophysiology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Felectrophysiology&trk=public_post-text), into a single computational database where provenance and reproducibility are baked in. Not just storage, but a live, queryable record of discovery.   Some companies chase the sun. [enurgen](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/enurgen?trk=public_post-text) decided to model it. The Ottawa-based deep-tech startup just secured a $4.1M seed round led by [BDC](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/bdc?trk=public_post-text), [Brightspark Ventures](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/brightspark?trk=public_post-text), and [Diagram](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/diagram-ventures?trk=public_post-text) Ventures, with [MaRS Investment Accelerator Fund](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/marsiaf?trk=public_post-text) joining the mix. It's the kind of funding move that tells you the energy game isn't just heating up, it's getting smarter. This one's a physics play wrapped in code, spun straight out of the [University of Ottawa](https://ca.linkedin.com/school/uottawa/?trk=public_post-text)'s [#SUNLAB](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsunlab&trk=public_post-text), where math meets [#megawatts](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmegawatts&trk=public_post-text) and the [#photons](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fphotons&trk=public_post-text) don't stand a chance. CEO & Co-Founder [Kibby Pollak](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/kibby-pollak?trk=public_post-text), a SUNLAB alum with engineering roots and startup grit, has teamed up with CTO & Co-Founder Dr. [Christopher E. Valdivia](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/christopher-e-valdivia?trk=public_post-text), whose PhD in Optoelectronics gives DUET, their flagship 3D finite-element engine, its pulse. Add Professor [Karin Hinzer](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/karin-hinzer-7860b52?trk=public_post-text), President & Co-Founder, the architect of SUNLAB itself, and Professor [Henry Schriemer](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/henry-schriemer-a021b025?trk=public_post-text), Co-Founder & Head of Research, whose academic fingerprints are all over the science, and you've got a founding team that's half lab coat, half laser beam. This isn't just software; it's a digital twin that can predict a [#solarplant](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsolarplant&trk=public_post-text)'s yield within 1% of real-world performance. That's not marketing. That's math with swagger. DUET, short for Dual-sided Energy Tracer, doesn't rely on AI guesswork. It runs [#physics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fphysics&trk=public_post-text)-first, from the cell to the gigawatt, mapping [#light](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flight&trk=public_post-text), [#terrain](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fterrain&trk=public_post-text), and [#weather](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fweather&trk=public_post-text) in obsessive detail. It's precision built for the people keeping the lights on: IPPs, EPCs, and asset managers who can't afford to be wrong. Enurgen's already working with global [#solaroperators](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsolaroperators&trk=public_post-text), validating models that could erase the industry's 7–13% performance gap, numbers that make CFOs and [#climatescientists](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fclimatescientists&trk=public_post-text) equally sleepless. Backed by over 7 years of R&D and 10+ years of SUNLAB credibility, Enurgen grew from a 6-person research spinoff to a 50-strong force by 2025. Their secret? Staying rooted in physics while scaling through software. While the rest of the market is busy training models, Enurgen is training accuracy. In solar, precision isn't a feature, it's survival. This $4.1M round isn't just capital, it's fuel. It powers DUET's evolution, global expansion, and integration with the tools that run solar's day-to-day grind. Pollak and Valdivia aren't chasing sunlight; they're engineering how the world measures it. In a $500M+ market where every watt counts, Enurgen isn't promising perfection, they're proving it, 1 photon at a time. results.title: Alina Rui Su, Michael Suswal, and Dr. Irina Conboy: The ...
results.content: 🧬 Can our microbiome shape how we feel? A new meta-analysis in npj Biofilms and Microbiomes by Kowal et al. (2025) provides the first systematic evidence linking microbiome diversity with psychological well-being. The findings are striking: individuals with a richer, more diverse microbiome tend to report higher life satisfaction and better emotional health. Notably, the bacterial phylum Bacillota showed a particularly strong association with well-being. At MusB Research, we’re passionate about uncovering the hidden microbial communities in our gut and exploring the secrets they hold in blood and urine — from physical health markers to mental resilience and overall wellness. We welcome collaborations! Reach out to us at info@musbresearch.com  to explore cutting-edge microbiome research [...] A recent study found that older adults living in areas with high levels of fine particulate matter (PM 2.5) had nearly 20% greater odds of more severe Alzheimer’s pathology at autopsy. At Elarin Health, our predictive platform integrates detailed environmental inputs—temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and air-quality metrics—through our collaboration with meteoblue, a global leader in environmental data analytics. This enables us to identify when fall risk is rising in real time. Our plan is to extend this same modeling approach to understand other physiologic impacts of environmental exposure, including early indicators of cognitive decline. By connecting environment, physiology, and behavior, we’re building insight into when risk emerges—not just what happens after it does. [...] A recent study found that older adults living in areas with high levels of fine particulate matter (PM 2.5) had nearly 20% greater odds of more severe Alzheimer’s pathology at autopsy. At Elarin Health, our predictive platform integrates detailed environmental inputs—temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and air-quality metrics—through our collaboration with meteoblue, a global leader in environmental data analytics. This enables us to identify when fall risk is rising in real time. Our plan is to extend this same modeling approach to understand other physiologic impacts of environmental exposure, including early indicators of cognitive decline. By connecting environment, physiology, and behavior, we’re building insight into when risk emerges—not just what happens after it does.

## More Relevant Posts

* [Elarin Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/elarin-health?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

  152 followers

  + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Felarin-health_what-the-air-you-breathe-may-be-doing-to-activity-7394399789896855552-FaEy&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

  A recent study found that older adults living in areas with high levels of fine particulate matter (PM 2.5) had nearly 20% greater odds of more severe Alzheimer’s pathology at autopsy. At [Elarin Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/elarin-health?trk=public_post-text), our predictive platform integrates detailed environmental inputs—temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and air-quality metrics—through our collaboration with [meteoblue](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/meteoblue?trk=public_post-text), a global leader in environmental data analytics. This enables us to identify when fall risk is rising in real time. Our plan is to extend this same modeling approach to understand other physiologic impacts of environmental exposure, including early indicators of cognitive decline. By connecting environment, physiology, and behavior, we’re building insight into when risk emerges—not just what happens after it does. Our plan is to extend this same modeling approach to understand other physiologic impacts of environmental exposure, including early indicators of cognitive decline. By connecting environment, physiology, and behavior, we’re building insight into when risk emerges—not just what happens after it does.   What if aging wasn’t something that had to happen to you- but something you could measure, manage, and even slow down? Longevity medicine is changing what it means to grow older.   As fellow Co-Founder of [Unlimited Life](https://www.linkedin.com/company/unlimited-life-movement?trk=public_post-text) Dr. Timothy Cook, MD, FRCPC, MPH, LCol (Ret'd), CD- one of the world’s foremost longevity experts- explains, we can now understand why you’re aging, and begin to turn off that process to help you stay biologically younger, longer.   It’s not science fiction. And when you can measure it, you can improve it.   Imagine optimizing not just your lifespan, but your healthspan- the number of years you live with vitality, clarity, and purpose.   That’s what [Unlimited Life](https://www.linkedin.com/company/unlimited-life-movement?trk=public_post-text) is about: using cutting-edge science to expand the human experience- not just to live longer, but to live healthier, and fulfilled.   🧬 Can our microbiome shape how we feel? A new meta-analysis in npj Biofilms and Microbiomes by Kowal et al. (2025) provides the first systematic evidence linking microbiome diversity with psychological well-being. The findings are striking: individuals with a richer, more diverse microbiome tend to report higher life satisfaction and better emotional health. Notably, the bacterial phylum Bacillota showed a particularly strong association with well-being. At MusB Research, we’re passionate about uncovering the hidden microbial communities in our gut and exploring the secrets they hold in blood and urine — from physical health markers to mental resilience and overall wellness. We welcome collaborations!   The Science Summit at Oxford this morning opened with remarks from Jim Mellon, Co-Founder of The Longevity Forum, and Lord Neil Mendoza, Provost of Oriel College. This year’s Summit is dedicated to leading researchers sharing insights across key areas of longevity science — from dementia and hormone health to air quality, sleep, and cellular reprogramming. Highlights so far: • Early intervention matters: The earliest biological measures taken in one’s 40s show the strongest correlations with cognitive health later in life — underscoring the importance of prevention at midlife. • Testosterone awareness: While not all men experience lower testosterone levels, recognising and addressing hormonal changes — through lifestyle or medical support — can make a significant difference to long-term wellbeing and quality of life.   🧬 The Biggest Misconception in Longevity Medicine is…….. When people hear longevity, they imagine cryotherapy chambers, IV drips, and biomarker dashboards lighting up like stock tickers. But that’s not longevity medicine. ⸻ What Longevity Really Is : True longevity medicine is not about chasing immortality - it’s about preserving human function as long as possible. It’s not about living forever. It’s about living fully. And yet, the biggest misconception persists: that longevity is about adding - more supplements, more devices, more data. In reality, the most powerful interventions are about subtracting: noise, inconsistency, inflammation, decision fatigue. The biology of longevity thrives on stability, not stimulation. ⸻ The Myth of the “Data-Driven Savior” We love numbers because they give us a sense of control. But no blood test can fix a lifestyle built on chaos. No biomarker can compensate for poor sleep, chronic stress, and isolation. Data doesn’t change your life — discipline does. The lab report is a mirror, not a solution. ⸻ The Real Longevity Stack Forget protocols that read like startup pitch decks. Here’s what science still can’t out-engineer: • Sleep: the most potent anti-aging therapy we have. • Movement: strength training is neuroprotective, not aesthetic. • Nutrition: mitochondria don’t understand trends; they understand consistency. • Stress: longevity collapses when cortisol rules the system. • Connection: people who age well, don’t age alone. You can’t outsource these. You can only build them. ⸻ The Shift That’s Coming Longevity isn’t a new branch of medicine. It’s the integration of everything we already know biology, psychology, technology, and behavior - into one unified system of preventive precision. The future isn’t in chasing exotic biomarkers. It’s in teaching behavior to catch up with biology. ⸻ A Thought If you knew your biological age today would it change how you live tomorrow? Because the real goal isn’t to extend lifespan. It’s to make every year you already have fuller and more functional.   How Modern Longevity Science Helps Us Live Better, Longer Honoured to be featured in [Longevitylive](https://www.linkedin.com/company/longevitylive?trk=public_post-text) magazine, where I share insights on how modern longevity science, regenerative medicine, and ancient wisdom converge to help us live better, longer. After more than 30 years in healthcare — from NHS frontline medicine to founding Levitas One — one principle has become impossible to ignore: 👉 Human biology thrives when we return to nature’s design. In this feature, I explore: ·      Why the current healthcare model is failing to address root causes ·      How symptoms are actually signals that guide us back to physiological balance ·      The science and philosophy behind the Levitas 8R Longevity Framework ·      The importance of cellular health, mitochondrial optimisation, metabolic repair, and nervous-system regulation ·      How removing interference — toxins, stress, emotional load — activates the body’s innate healing intelligence Longevity is more than extending lifespan. It’s about improving healthspan, restoring vitality, and empowering individuals to reclaim ownership of their physical, mental, and emotional wellbeing. 👉 My mission at Levitas One remains clear: To redefine what health optimisation can look like when science, nature and human potential work together. I hope this conversation encourages more people to question the traditional healthcare narrative and embrace a more integrated, preventative and regenerative approach to health.
