  “The next evolution of wellness isn’t just measuring physiology — it’s activating transformation. While clinics today can track heart rate, breathing patterns, or brain activity, EN7HEOS provides the missing layer: a guided emotional technology designed to generate measurable energetic and emotional change within the client. Through structured interventions, personalized guidance, and an evidence-based process, EN7HEOS complements existing biofeedback tools by addressing the emotional cause — not just the physiological signal. It’s where data meets consciousness.” If your clinic already measures what’s happening in the body, now you can guide what happens within.



  [1](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnatalia-reyes-retana-264966116_the-next-evolution-of-wellness-isnt-just-activity-7385747545555116033-DxQm&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

  [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnatalia-reyes-retana-264966116_the-next-evolution-of-wellness-isnt-just-activity-7385747545555116033-DxQm&trk=public_post_like-cta)   [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnatalia-reyes-retana-264966116_the-next-evolution-of-wellness-isnt-just-activity-7385747545555116033-DxQm&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

  To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnatalia-reyes-retana-264966116_the-next-evolution-of-wellness-isnt-just-activity-7385747545555116033-DxQm&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)
* [Nayan Sharma](https://in.linkedin.com/in/nayan-sharma-37484b172?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

  + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnayan-sharma-37484b172_the-epidemiological-and-toxicological-intersection-activity-7390938302230556672-XJjS&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

  Clean air is brain care. A recent review published in the National Library of Medicine reveals compelling evidence that long-term exposure to fine particulate matter (PM2.5) and nitrogen dioxide (NO2) significantly increases dementia risk. The mechanisms are clear; oxidative stress, neuroinflammation and vascular injury, all leading to accelerated cognitive decline. What we breathe today could shape how our brains age tomorrow. This shifts the narrative: air quality isn’t just an environmental issue, it’s a neuroprotective priority. For policymakers, city planners and health leaders, cleaner air isn’t optional, it’s a public health strategy for cognitive longevity. (Ref: [https://lnkd.in/gYswkiCp](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FgYswkiCp&urlhash=5hbg&trk=public_post-text)) [#AirQuality](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fairquality&trk=public_post-text) [#BrainHealth](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrainhealth&trk=public_post-text) [#DementiaPrevention](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdementiaprevention&trk=public_post-text) [#PublicHealth](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpublichealth&trk=public_post-text) [#UrbanHealth](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Furbanhealth&trk=public_post-text) [#Neuroscience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fneuroscience&trk=public_post-text)

  [The Epidemiological and Toxicological Intersection of Air Pollution and Dementia   pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpmc%2Encbi%2Enlm%2Enih%2Egov%2Farticles%2FPMC12513895%2F&urlhash=Eeyu&trk=public_post_feed-article-content)

  [7](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnayan-sharma-37484b172_the-epidemiological-and-toxicological-intersection-activity-7390938302230556672-XJjS&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

  [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnayan-sharma-37484b172_the-epidemiological-and-toxicological-intersection-activity-7390938302230556672-XJjS&trk=public_post_like-cta)   [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnayan-sharma-37484b172_the-epidemiological-and-toxicological-intersection-activity-7390938302230556672-XJjS&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

  To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnayan-sharma-37484b172_the-epidemiological-and-toxicological-intersection-activity-7390938302230556672-XJjS&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)
* [Wellness Eternal](https://www.linkedin.com/company/wellness-eternal?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

  171 followers

  + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fwellness-eternal_biohackingindex-therasage-redlighttherapy-activity-7393980898456788994-APiA&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

  We talk a lot about hydration, nutrients, and recovery routines — but there’s a deeper layer most wellness conversations miss: Protein folding. It’s the invisible step that determines how well your cells repair, think, signal, and age. Therasage just published a beautifully clear explanation on the Biohacking Index breaking down how light (especially red & near-infrared) powers this cellular process — and how hydration and mitochondrial energy shape protein structure at the most fundamental level. This isn’t hype — it’s the underlying mechanism that makes light therapy meaningful. Grateful to see brands bringing science-first clarity to the wellness space. 🔗 Read it on the Biohacking Index. [https://lnkd.in/eaj-a3Uq](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Feaj-a3Uq&urlhash=Pg8r&trk=public_post-text) [#BiohackingIndex](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiohackingindex&trk=public_post-text) [#Therasage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftherasage&trk=public_post-text) [#RedLightTherapy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fredlighttherapy&trk=public_post-text) [#CellularHealth](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcellularhealth&trk=public_post-text) [#LongevityScience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flongevityscience&trk=public_post-text)

News Archive [...] UC Berkeley |Office of the Vice Chancellor for Research

Intellectual Property & Industry Research Alliances

# Generation Lab

Generation Lab logo

### Brief Description

Generation Lab provides the first and only platform that measures the effectiveness of longevity interventions to identify what works. Co-founded by pre-eminent aging and longevity researcher Dr. Irina Conboy from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines an easy-to-use test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease before symptoms arise. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions to increase health, wellness, and longevity; and Generation Lab uniquely measures the effectiveness of these over time.

[Intellectual Property & Industry Research Alliances](/ "Home")

# Generation Lab

[![Generation Lab logo](https://ipira.berkeley.edu/sites/default/files/styles/openberkeley_image_full/public/generation-lab.jpg?itok=VEz506VC&timestamp=1721254104)](https://www.generationlab.com/)

### Brief Description

Generation Lab provides the first and only platform that measures the effectiveness of longevity interventions to identify what works. Co-founded by pre-eminent aging and longevity researcher Dr. Irina Conboy from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines an easy-to-use test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease before symptoms arise. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions to increase health, wellness, and longevity; and Generation Lab uniquely measures the effectiveness of these over time.

Generation Lab is so named because the cofounding team spans three generations.

#### Inventors

Irina Conboy, Colin Sinner, Connor David Luellen, Michael Conboy

#### Company Founders

Alina Su (UC Berkeley BS, Bioengineering and Biomedical Engineering), UC Berkeley Professor of Bioengineering Irina Conboy, and Michael Suswal

#### Timeline

* **2023**. Company founded

results.title: UT Austin launches Innovation Labs for biomedical innovation
results.content: Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. Generation Lab just raised $11M in seed funding, led by Accel, marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by Alina Rui Su, Dr. Irina Conboy, and Michael Suswal, Generation Lab isn't just talking about #aging; they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, Harvard Medical School PhD, University of California, Berkeley bioengineering alum, Forbes 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped [...] professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped #agingbiology on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of Standard AI and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your #biologicalage, it measures it. With over 460 #epigenetic markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your #brain, #heart, #liver, or #immunesystem really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year #brainage reversal using MUSE #Stemcell treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, [...] featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, Giannis Antetokounmpo's BYL Ventures, and Simu Liu's Markham Valley Ventures, pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on #longevity as the next frontier. When George Church, Aubrey de Grey, and Dr. Adeel Khan, MD are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline. In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade #diagnostics built to turn #healthspan into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't. The play here isn't
results.raw_content:
# UT Austin launches Innovation Labs for biomedical innovation

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[Andrew J. Maas](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewjmaas?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

* [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fandrewjmaas_ut-opens-off-campus-lab-space-for-private-activity-7392328431331467264-P7uv&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Today marks a major milestone at [The University of Texas at Austin](https://www.linkedin.com/school/theuniversityoftexasataustin-/?trk=public_post-text) in our mission to accelerate biomedical innovation: UT Innovation Labs has officially launched! This new off-campus lab space is designed to bridge the gap between academic research and commercial application, providing a dynamic environment where private-sector partners can collaborate with UT researchers to bring life sciences technologies to market faster and more effectively. As part of UT’s growing commitment to translational research, the [Discovery to Impact](https://www.linkedin.com/company/discovery-to-impact?trk=public_post-text) Innovation Labs will serve as a catalyst for commercializing biomedical technologies, right here in Austin, a city already known for its vibrant tech and health ecosystem. A huge shoutout to the incredible Technology Development team: [Michael Yost, Ph.D.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-j-yost-phd?trk=public_post-text), [Joseph Skraba](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joseph-skraba-537a73?trk=public_post-text), [Jessica Momb, Ph.D.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jmomb?trk=public_post-text), and [Jennifer Blossom](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jennifer-blossom?trk=public_post-text). Your support, guidance, vision and dedication helped make this launch a reality. Your team truly exemplifies the power of strategic partnerships and innovation-driven leadership. This is more than just a new space - it's a new chapter for UT and for the future of life sciences commercialization. [#UTInnovationLabs](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Futinnovationlabs&trk=public_post-text) [#BiomedicalInnovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiomedicalinnovation&trk=public_post-text) [#TechnologyTransfer](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftechnologytransfer&trk=public_post-text) [#AustinTech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faustintech&trk=public_post-text) [#LifeSciences](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flifesciences&trk=public_post-text) [#Commercialization](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcommercialization&trk=public_post-text) [#UTResearch](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Futresearch&trk=public_post-text) [#InnovationEcosystem](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finnovationecosystem&trk=public_post-text)

[Discovery to Impact](https://www.linkedin.com/company/discovery-to-impact?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

1,701 followers

Big news for Austin’s life sciences sector! 🤘 Discovery to Impact has officially opened UT Innovation Labs, a state-of-the-art, 10,000-square-foot wet lab designed to accelerate breakthroughs in health care and life sciences. As part of our mission to create spaces where innovators can collaborate with entrepreneurial ventures, UT Innovation Labs strengthens [The University of Texas at Austin](https://www.linkedin.com/school/theuniversityoftexasataustin-/?trk=public_post_reshare-text)'s commitment to advancing life science technology development and fueling Austin’s growing innovation ecosystem. The new labs connect startups with UT’s world-class research facilities, faculty expertise, and entrepreneurial support, providing the resources and community to move discoveries from the lab to the world. Read more at UT News: [https://lnkd.in/edKH2xAn](https://lnkd.in/edKH2xAn?trk=public_post_reshare-text) [#WhatStartsHere](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fwhatstartshere&trk=public_post_reshare-text) [#DiscoverytoImpact](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdiscoverytoimpact&trk=public_post_reshare-text)

  The Cambridge Science & Technology Cluster will never have seen anything like it - a self-contained township of research facilities and new homes that will put the UK, right left and centre of international genomics and complementary life science research. [Robert Evans](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/robert-evans-a38a9720a?trk=public_post-text), CEO, [Wellcome Genome Campus](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/wellcomegenomecampus?trk=public_post-text) has revealed to Business Weekly the full and impressive scale of a development that will revolutionise Cambridge and UK research and wow the world. The Wellcome Genome Campus is already a globally recognised, established research hub but is now expanding for the future to support the UK's position as a world leader in science and technology. Evans says that over the next few years, the Campus will increase in size from 125 acres to 440 acres, “to accommodate a growing community of leading institutes, companies and organisations in genomics, biodata, health data, translation and data science.” The expansion builds on the international standing and achievements of the [Wellcome Sanger Institute](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/wellcome-sanger-institute?trk=public_post-text), the [European Bioinformatics Institute | EMBL-EBI](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/ebi?trk=public_post-text) and the Government’s plans for a new Health Data Research Service (HDRS). Evans says: “Technology, data, and AI are revolutionising life sciences, presenting new ways to tackle some of humanity’s greatest challenges. We want to provide the facilities, infrastructure and environment to attract and support the brightest minds in grasping these opportunities. “The approved plans include 1.6 million square feet for R&D, laboratories and commercial spaces, suitable for organisations of all shapes and sizes. The new space could accommodate up to 250 organisations, or even more, depending on their size. “We are currently finalising the first construction contracts and will announce shortly the identity of our main contractors.   The Cambridge Science & Technology Cluster will never have seen anything like it - a self-contained township of research facilities and new homes that will put the UK, right left and centre of international genomics and complementary life science research. [Robert Evans](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/robert-evans-a38a9720a?trk=public_post_reshare-text), CEO, [Wellcome Genome Campus](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/wellcomegenomecampus?trk=public_post_reshare-text) has revealed to Business Weekly the full and impressive scale of a development that will revolutionise Cambridge and UK research and wow the world. The Wellcome Genome Campus is already a globally recognised, established research hub but is now expanding for the future to support the UK's position as a world leader in science and technology. Evans says that over the next few years, the Campus will increase in size from 125 acres to 440 acres, “to accommodate a growing community of leading institutes, companies and organisations in genomics, biodata, health data, translation and data science.” The expansion builds on the international standing and achievements of the [Wellcome Sanger Institute](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/wellcome-sanger-institute?trk=public_post_reshare-text), the [European Bioinformatics Institute | EMBL-EBI](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/ebi?trk=public_post_reshare-text) and the Government’s plans for a new Health Data Research Service (HDRS). Evans says: “Technology, data, and AI are revolutionising life sciences, presenting new ways to tackle some of humanity’s greatest challenges. We want to provide the facilities, infrastructure and environment to attract and support the brightest minds in grasping these opportunities. “The approved plans include 1.6 million square feet for R&D, laboratories and commercial spaces, suitable for organisations of all shapes and sizes. The new space could accommodate up to 250 organisations, or even more, depending on their size. “We are currently finalising the first construction contracts and will announce shortly the identity of our main contractors. We are about to start on site with our first new buildings, so it is a very exciting time. “The plans also include 1500 homes, for Campus workers and their families, new retail and food and drink, sports, health and fitness uses, a nursery, primary school, play area and other community facilities – all connected by new, inclusive routes and civic spaces, parks, gardens and bridges. “Wellcome is undertaking one of the largest contemporary investments in the UK’s life sciences and technology infrastructure, committing hundreds of millions at a time when others appear to be stepping back. “The whole expansion project may take around 10 years, depending on a number of factors including Government support for life sciences and market demand. “The Campus also needs public transport. We will be working with central and local Government, the Mayor and others to make the case. In our view, it is regrettable and a missed opportunity that the planned CSET does not come to the Campus. In our view, it still could and should.” [https://lnkd.in/e7x4xnBz](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fe7x4xnBz&urlhash=MSUz&trk=public_post_reshare-text)

  Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) just raised $11M in seed funding, led by [Accel](https://www.linkedin.com/company/accel-vc?trk=public_post-text), marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text), Dr. Irina Conboy, and [Michael Suswal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-suswal-51b45421?trk=public_post-text), Generation Lab isn't just talking about [#aging](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faging&trk=public_post-text); they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) PhD, [University of California, Berkeley](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uc-berkeley/?trk=public_post-text) bioengineering alum, [Forbes](https://www.linkedin.com/company/forbes-magazine?trk=public_post-text) 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose [#research](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fresearch&trk=public_post-text) on plasma dilution flipped [#agingbiology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fagingbiology&trk=public_post-text) on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of [Standard AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/standard-ai?trk=public_post-text) and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your [#biologicalage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiologicalage&trk=public_post-text), it measures it. With over 460 [#epigenetic](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fepigenetic&trk=public_post-text) markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your [#brain](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrain&trk=public_post-text), [#heart](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fheart&trk=public_post-text), [#liver](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fliver&trk=public_post-text), or [#immunesystem](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimmunesystem&trk=public_post-text) really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year [#brainage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrainage&trk=public_post-text) reversal using MUSE [#Stemcell](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstemcell&trk=public_post-text) treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring [Samsung Next](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samsung-next?trk=public_post-text), [Zone2](https://www.linkedin.com/company/z2-zone2?trk=public_post-text), Steve Aoki's [Aoki Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/aokilabs?trk=public_post-text), Giannis Antetokounmpo's [BYL Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bylventures?trk=public_post-text), and [Simu Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simu-liu-47946a2b1?trk=public_post-text)'s [Markham Valley Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/markhamvalley?trk=public_post-text), pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on [#longevity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flongevity&trk=public_post-text) as the next frontier. When George Church, [Aubrey de Grey](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aubrey-de-grey-24260b?trk=public_post-text), and [Dr. Adeel Khan, MD](https://ae.linkedin.com/in/dradeelkhan-md?trk=public_post-text) are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline. In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade [#diagnostics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdiagnostics&trk=public_post-text) built to turn [#healthspan](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhealthspan&trk=public_post-text) into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't. The play here isn't immortality, it's accountability. Knowing how your organs are aging before symptoms show up changes the entire conversation around healthcare. Generation Lab isn't selling forever; they're selling control. That's the kind of disruption Accel just paid attention to.
